title: Richly Spiced Argentinian Beef Empanadas
description: A funky and juicy Argentinian dish featuring gochujang and candy, with anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients, these empanadas are a nostalgic treat. The combination of spices and sweet elements creates a unique flavor profile.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, cook the ground beef over medium-high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
Stir in the gochujang, turmeric, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Fold in the candy pieces and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Roll out the empanada dough and cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of the beef mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half over the filling and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little bit of water and bake for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
